methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $90M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $400M - $2.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 36.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: ClearSpace
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series A), post-money $400M

data.narrative:
Observable Space, founded by Dan Roelker and Rick Hedrick, is a Series A space and aerospace company that has raised $90M to date. Valuation analysis relies on two applicable methods triangulated against a peer set of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies (ClearSpace, Cowboy Space Corporation, Aalyria, and Hubble Network) with valuations ranging $400M–$2.0B, alongside five public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) trading at a median 36.9x price-to-sales.

Stage-progression methodology applies a standard 2.0x Series A step-up multiplier to the $90M capital raised proxy, yielding $180M. Peer-median valuation across the four identified private companies produces $850M, reflecting the upper-stage (Series B) skew within the comparable set and a wide dispersion ($400M–$2.0B). Public-comp anchoring via revenue multiples cannot be applied; Observable Space's revenue is not disclosed, a typical constraint for early-stage private companies.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $153M (low) to $977M (high), with a median of $515M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around stage positioning and peer heterogeneity—particularly the inclusion of later-stage (Series B) comparables that command higher multiples. Key uncertainties include revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and technical execution risk typical of frontier aerospace ventures.
